2605.06347.title: Human-AI Co-Evolution and Epistemic Collapse: A Dynamical Systems Perspective
2605.06347.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are reshaping how knowledge is produced, with increasing reliance on AI systems for generation, summarization, and reasoning. While prior work has studied cognitive offloading in humans and model collapse in recursive training, these effects are typically considered in isolation. We propose a unified perspective: humans and language models form a coupled dynamical system linked by a feedback loop of usage, generation, and retraining. We introduce a minimal model with three variables -- human cognition, data quality, and model capability -- and show that this feedback can give rise to distinct dynamical regimes. Our analysis identifies three regimes: co-evolutionary enhancement, fragile equilibrium, and degenerative convergence. Through a simple simulation, we demonstrate that increasing reliance on AI can induce a transition toward a low-diversity, suboptimal equilibrium. From an information-theoretic perspective, this transition corresponds to an emergent information bottleneck in the human-AI loop, where entropy reduction reflects loss of diversity and support under closed-loop feedback rather than beneficial compression. These results suggest that the trajectory of AI systems is shaped not only by model design, but by the dynamics of human-AI co-evolution.
2605.06347.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）正在重塑知識的產生方式，對於生成、摘要和推理的依賴越來越多。雖然之前的研究已經探討了人類的認知外包和遞歸訓練中的模型崩潰，但這些效應通常是孤立考慮的。我們提出了一個統一的觀點：人類和語言模型形成了一個通過使用、生成和再訓練的反饋循環相連的耦合動態系統。我們引入了一個包含三個變量的最小模型——人類認知、數據質量和模型能力——並展示這種反饋可以產生不同的動態範疇。我們的分析確定了三個範疇：共同進化增強、脆弱平衡和退化收斂。通過一個簡單的模擬，我們展示了對AI的依賴增加可以引發向低多樣性、次優平衡的轉變。從信息論的角度來看，這一轉變對應於人類-AI循環中的一個新興信息瓶頸，其中熵的減少反映了在閉環反饋下多樣性的喪失和支持，而不是有益的壓縮。這些結果表明，AI系統的發展軌跡不僅受到模型設計的影響，還受到人類-AI共同進化的動態影響。
2605.06347.author: Xuening Wu et.al.
2605.06347.authors: Xuening Wu, Yanlan Kang, Qianya Xu, Kexuan Xie, Jiaqi Mi, Honggang Wang, Yubin Liu, Zeping Chen
